subject: Geography
title: Secret Maps of Empire
description: A 60-second lesson on Secret Maps of Empire.

hook.text: Portugal classified its sea charts as state secrets. Leaking one was punishable by death.

buildup.text: Accurate maps of trade routes were military advantages worth more than gold.

discovery.text: Spain and Portugal divided the unknown world between them using a line on a map in 1494.

twist.text: Spies routinely stole maps from rival empires. Some were planted with false coastlines.

climax.text: Cartography was an arms race—whoever had the best maps controlled the seas.

punchline.text: Maps were weapons. The best ones were top secret.

quiz.question: Why did Portugal classify its sea charts as state secrets?
options: Accurate maps gave military and trade advantages
They were too expensive to copy
